<observation>
  <type>decision</type>
  <title>Cross-model AI strategy v4 - canonical corpus architecture</title>
  <subtitle>Comprehensive architecture defining git-based markdown canonical corpus as product with dual-repo strategy and claude-mem ingestion</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Core principle: canonical corpus IS the product - distilled knowledge tracked in git, not raw code/sessions, with markdown + git as foundation and all other components (CLI/MCP/bridge/index) as deployment infrastructure</fact>
    <fact>Dual-repository architecture: AI-canonical (personal, GitHub jiunchiwang/ai-canonical) for general knowledge, AI-canonical-corp (private, igs-jiunchiwang/ai-canonical-corp) for corporate IP with per-account authentication URLs</fact>
    <fact>Seven-phase implementation plan: Phase 0 (current state), Phase 1 (repo creation with secret hooks), Phase 2 (skills/steering migration with sync.ps1), Phase 3 (mcp-memory bridge), Phase 4 (FTS5 retrieval), Phase 5 (review workflow with claude-mem ingestion via tools/ingest-claude-mem.mjs), Phase 6 (evals), Phase 7 (multi-machine onboarding)</fact>
    <fact>Claude-mem integration strategy: claude-mem serves as Claude Code ephemeral session memory (~/.claude-mem with SQLite claude-mem.db and ChromaDB), with watermark-based ingestion extracting observations into canonical facts/ for review, NOT bulk migration</fact>
    <fact>Facts schema uses frontmatter with id, type (fact/preference/decision/procedure/warning), domain (general/slot/other), created/updated dates, tags, source, confidence, and wiki-style links</fact>
    <fact>Progress checkpoint: Phases 0-6 complete (249 observations ingested from claude-mem, 24 skills deployed via sync.ps1, dual repos established, mcp-memory bridge operational with hybrid FTS5+embedding+rerank, evals specs created), Phase 7 pending</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The primary session created the definitive v4 cross-model AI strategy document consolidating learnings from v1/v2/v3. The strategy centers on a fundamental principle: the canonical corpus of distilled knowledge is the product itself, not the AI tools that consume it. All knowledge is stored as markdown in git repositories, with strict distilled-only policy (no raw code, transcripts, or secrets). The architecture uses two repositories: a personal GitHub repo for general-purpose knowledge and a separate corporate repo for IP-sensitive content, both using per-account authentication URLs. Skills and steering configurations deploy to both Kiro (~/.kiro) and Claude Code (~/.claude) via Windows NTFS junctions managed by sync.ps1. The memory system has three layers: claude-mem (ephemeral session memory with SQLite DB and ChromaDB for Claude Code), mcp-memory bridge (TypeScript MCP server with hybrid FTS5+embedding retrieval), and AIMemory canonical storage (git-tracked markdown facts and wiki). A watermark-based ingestion pipeline (tools/ingest-claude-mem.mjs) extracts claude-mem observations into proposals for selective canonical storage, avoiding bulk migration. The seven-phase implementation plan shows Phases 0-6 complete with 249 observations ingested, 24 skills deployed, dual repos operational, and evaluation frameworks established. Phase 7 (multi-machine onboarding via bootstrap.ps1) remains pending. The document includes detailed schemas, risk mitigation strategies, current state inventory mapping 16 Kiro skills, 18 Claude skills, 26 commands, 15 agents, and complete file structure specifications.</narrative>
